commands.author: jason-allen-oneal
commands.command: p1 addressed in 82dc858e89. `readrootjsonobjectsync` now only caches when `params` is exactly `{ rootdir, relativepath }`. any extra key — `rejecthardlinks`, `maxbytes`, `rootrealpath`, or any future fs-safe option — short-circuits to `rawreadrootjsonobjectsync` before touching the cache. safe by construction against fs-safe adding new options later. regression test in `src/infra/json-files.test.ts`: populates the cache with the option-free shape, asserts a same-shape call returns the same object reference (cache hit), then asserts a call with `rejecthardlinks: true` returns a fresh object (cache bypassed). the earlier commit `7b54ab9bbc` is a separate fix — it routes the legacy `loadjsonfile` (`src/infra/json-file.ts`) to the raw uncached reader so callers that own their own cache (subagent registry store, cli-credentials, auth-profiles, doctor-auth-*) don't double-stat or risk stale auth across re-auth.

target.title: fix(tui): handle German AltGr input
target.body:
## Summary
- ignore Kitty printable key-release events in the TUI custom editor so release events cannot duplicate text
- decode German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable input when terminals report Ctrl+Alt plus a base-layout key
- add regression coverage for AltGr @/€, ordinary Alt-modified input, and printable release events

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live German keyboard / Kitty-terminal smoke test; this environment does not have that keyboard layout attached.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: TUI input should accept German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable characters such as `@` and `€`, while ignoring printable key-release events so releases do not duplicate text. This covers issue #48897 in `src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts`.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux 6.19.14+kali-amd64 isolated worktree at PR head `6dfa6f45a0`; Node v22.22.2; pnpm 11.1.0. The smoke used a pseudo-terminal, raw stdin bytes, and the real `CustomEditor.handleInput(...)` path with `@earendil-works/pi-tui` key decoding.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `git switch --detach origin/main` in the isolated proof worktree, then ran a Python PTY script that spawned `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, constructed `CustomEditor`, set stdin raw, and wrote Kitty CSI-u bytes to the PTY.
  2. Repeated the same PTY/raw-stdin script at PR head `origin/pr-83947`.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the PTY smoke:
  ```
  current main input bytes: ^[[64::113;7u ^[[8364::101;7u ^[[214;1u ^[[214;1:3u
  current main RESULT {"text":"ÖÖ","length":2}
  PR head input bytes:    ^[[64::113;7u ^[[8364::101;7u ^[[214;1u ^[[214;1:3u
  PR head RESULT {"text":"@€Ö","length":3}

  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts
  ✓ tui src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts (5 tests) 89ms
  Test Files 1 passed (1)
  Tests 5 passed (5)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The same raw Kitty CSI-u stream that produced `ÖÖ` on current main produced `@€Ö` on the PR branch: AltGr `@` and `€` were inserted, the ordinary `Ö` press was inserted once, and the `Ö` release event was ignored instead of duplicating the character.
- What was not tested: Did not test a physical German keyboard or the live Kitty terminal application; this environment does not have that keyboard layout attached. The smoke exercised the exact CSI-u bytes through a PTY/raw-stdin path instead.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#48897
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
